summary: Soaring Dark Star, gorgeous Looks Like Rain & tight groove‑filled jams
blurb: The Copenhagen show delivers a monster Dark Star and a pedal‑steel‑laden Looks Like Rain that sparkle. Tight rhythm work drives Truckin', Brown Eyed Women and an unforgettable Good Lovin' → Caution sandwich. Overall the performance is consistently high‑energy and beautifully locked in.
review: On April 14, 1972 at Tivoli Theater the Dead were firing on all cylinders during their European ’72 run. Jerry Garcia’s lead work is nothing short of telepathic – his soaring Dark Star solo and the rare pedal‑steel flourish on Looks Like Rain are repeatedly called “phenomenal” and “gorgeous.” Keith Godchaux’s piano provides a bright, articulate backdrop while Bill Kreutzmann lays down a funky, driving pulse that fuels the relentless groove. The setlist flows like a well‑oiled machine: a punchy first set rolls into a second‑set marathon where Dark Star segues into Sugar Magnolia, then erupts into the legendary Good Lovin' → Caution sandwich before winding down with a seamless Not Fade Away > Goin’ Down The Road Feeling Bad > Not Fade Away trio. With only a handful of minor mix quirks on a few deep‑cut numbers, this concert stands as one of the high points of the spring ’72 tour and is essential listening for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All seven recordings are uniformly positive, repeatedly praising the Dark Star, Looks Like Rain pedal‑steel, and the Good Lovin' sandwich. No reviewer mentions consistent performance flaws; only isolated song‑level criticisms appear. The consensus of “legendary,” “soaring,” and “tight groove‑filled” language across multiple sources warrants a strong positive sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture, crisp and clear with no drop‑outs; the Miller repitched version is praised as the clearest of all SBD sources.

key_highlights: Soaring Dark Star with telepathic guitar work
Pedal‑steel magic on Looks Like Rain
Epic Good Lovin' → Caution sandwich and tight jam flow
Crystal‑clear soundboard quality that captures every nuance

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers telepathic solos, a soaring Dark Star and gorgeous pedal‑steel on Looks Like Rain; his guitar tone is described as perfect throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Provides sweet, punchy bass lines that lock in with the band, highlighted on Casey Jones and noted for a memorable Philo Stomp groove.
band_performance.Bob: Offers solid vocal delivery and tight rhythm work; verses on Truckin' are spot‑on and he blends seamlessly with Jerry’s leads.
band_performance.Billy: Funky, powerful drumming that drives the jams; his cymbal work is urgent and propels the high‑energy second set.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
